templates.name: template_active_inference
templates.title: template_active_inference
templates.use_when: Use this template when **several independent research tracks must compose into one manuscript whose claims stay consistent where the tracks overlap** — here: a closed-form analytical oracle, a pymdp simulation harness, a Lean formalization boundary, and shared GNN/ontology notation.

templates.name: template_autoresearch_project
templates.title: template_autoresearch_project
templates.use_when: Use this template when you need a **bounded, offline AutoResearch loop**: deterministic ML candidate evaluation over a fixed local dataset, with evidence-linked claims, machine-readable ledgers, artifact-integrity manifests, and deferred human-review gates.

templates.name: template_code_project
templates.title: Code Project — Optimization Research Exemplar
templates.use_when: Use this template for **code-driven computational research**: algorithms in `src/`, numerical experiments with deterministic seeds, automated publication-quality figures, and a manuscript that reports the computed results.

templates.name: template_madlib
templates.title: Template Madlib — Deterministic Token-Injection Exemplar
templates.use_when: Use this template when you need **configuration-driven manuscript generation with auditable token provenance, conditional section structure, explicit/default field visibility, failure-boundary reporting, and a reviewer-visible authoring contract**: lexicon categories, section titles, narrative moves, method steps, design principles, pipeline phases, evaluation criteria, QA probes, failure modes, authoring obligations, visualization controls, audit rules, and slots are declared in YAML; `src/` deterministically expands those declarations; and the manuscript receives large-grain `{{TOKEN}}` bodies only after the source code has generated the supporting artifacts.

templates.name: template_newspaper
templates.title: The Triplicate — `template_newspaper`
templates.use_when: Use this template when you need **data-driven, large-format print layout**: multi-page broadsheets/tabloids with precise column geometry, typography-first constraints, and strict content/engine separation (YAML editions in `content/`, pure-Python ReportLab engine in `src/`).

templates.name: template_prose_project
templates.title: template_prose_project
templates.use_when: Use this template for **manuscript-focused editorial pipelines**: readability gates (Flesch-Kincaid bands), structural checking, citation/BibTeX validation, and prose-quality review workflows where the document itself is the artifact under test.

templates.name: template_autoscientists
templates.title: AutoScientists — Coordination-Mechanism Testbed Exemplar
templates.use_when: Use this template when you need to **isolate and measure agent-coordination mechanisms**: coordinated teams vs single-thread baselines under matched budgets, deterministic ablation studies, and honest per-mechanism effect reporting (including nulls).

templates.name: template_sia
templates.title: template_sia
templates.use_when: Use this template when you need a **self-improvement-agent evaluation harness**: Meta → Target → Feedback generation loops, public/private task splits to detect overfitting, deterministic fixture replay for testability, and fail-closed loop validation.

templates.name: template_template
templates.title: Template: Meta-Research Project
templates.use_when: Use this template when your research subject is **the repository itself** — programmatic introspection of architecture, pipeline DAGs, module inventories, and security layers, rendered as a manuscript whose every metric is computed live (autopoietic: the paper regenerates itself from the code it describes).

templates.name: template_textbook
templates.title: The Template Textbook — Modular, Fillable Book Scaffold
templates.use_when: Use this template for **book-length manuscripts**: parts → chapters → labs → question banks declared in a single `config.yaml`, with auto-numbering, deterministic figure/diagram generation, and structural-contract tests that keep hundreds of pages from drifting.
